items.title: IP Hall of Fame inductees for 2026 announced
items.title_zh: 2026年IP名人堂入选者名单公布
items.title_en: IP Hall of Fame inductees for 2026 announced
items.summary: William ‘Bill’ Cornish, Roberto Dini and Justice Prathiba M Singh are the outstanding individuals selected for induction this year
items.ai_summary_zh: IAM Media宣布2026年知识产权名人堂入选者，包括William ‘Bill’ Cornish、Roberto Dini及Prathiba M Singh法官。该奖项旨在表彰对全球知识产权领域做出杰出贡献的个人。作为行业权威媒体，IAM通过此榜单确立行业标杆，反映当前IP界对法律理论、实务操作及司法公正的高度认可，是观察国际IP精英圈层的重要风向标。
items.ai_insight_zh: 中国IP从业者应关注此类国际荣誉背后的评价标准，如学术深度与司法影响力。虽非直接法规变动，但入选者往往主导规则解释方向。建议企业加强与国际顶尖专家的交流，提升合规与诉讼策略的理论高度，以应对日益复杂的跨境IP争议。

items.title: Exhibition in Portugal explores innovation in firefighting
items.title_zh: EPO在葡萄牙举办展览探索消防领域技术创新
items.title_en: Exhibition in Portugal explores innovation in firefighting
items.summary: The EPO’s Technology vs Fire exhibition opens in Sintra, Portugal, showcasing innovation in wildfire prevention, firefighting and recovery. It highlights how patent information and smart search tools support real-world solutions.
items.ai_summary_zh: 欧洲专利局（EPO）与葡萄牙工业产权局（INPI）合作，在辛特拉举办“科技对抗火灾”展览。该展览旨在展示森林防火、扑救及恢复方面的创新技术，并强调专利信息及智能搜索工具如何支持现实解决方案。EPO主席安东尼奥·坎皮诺斯指出，专利技术信息是技术开发的关键驱动力，有助于加速防火领域的创新进程。这是继希腊和斯洛文尼亚后的第三站展览，将持续至5月29日。
items.ai_insight_zh: 中国企业应关注EPO推出的防火技术智能搜索平台，利用Espacenet免费资源进行全球专利情报检索与分析。这有助于规避侵权风险，识别技术空白点，并为进入欧洲市场的绿色技术创新提供合规指引与研发灵感。

items.title: Meet the finalists in the 2026 European Inventor Award
items.title_zh: 2026年欧洲发明家奖12位入围者揭晓
items.title_en: Meet the finalists in the 2026 European Inventor Award
items.summary: Twelve finalists for the 2026 European Inventor Award span biotechnology, healthcare, renewable energy, semiconductors, digital tech and quantum. Winners will be revealed on 2 July.
items.ai_summary_zh: 欧洲专利局（EPO）公布2026年欧洲发明家奖12位入围者，涵盖生物技术、医疗、可再生能源、半导体及量子科技等领域。获奖者将于7月2日柏林典礼上公布，其中大众奖由陪审团与公众投票各占50%决定，公众每日可投三票。终身成就奖得主将于6月10日宣布。此次入围者来自意大利、瑞士、希腊等多个欧洲国家及非欧洲地区，展示了应对全球挑战的创新技术。
items.ai_insight_zh: 该奖项凸显欧洲对硬科技与绿色创新的重视。中国企业应关注入围技术在半导体、量子计算等领域的专利布局趋势，借鉴其国际化研发合作模式。同时，可利用此类国际平台提升品牌影响力，注意规避相关领域的知识产权风险，加强在欧洲市场的专利预警与合规管理。

items.title: EPO President delivers keynote at INTA Annual Meeting in London
items.title_zh: EPO主席在INTA伦敦年会主旨演讲：倡导IP整体战略
items.title_en: EPO President delivers keynote at INTA Annual Meeting in London
items.summary: EPO President Campinos delivered a keynote at the 2026 INTA Annual Meeting in London on 5 May, calling for a more holistic approach to intellectual property in which patents and trademarks work together.
items.ai_summary_zh: 2026年5月，EPO主席Campinos在伦敦INTA年会发表主旨演讲，呼吁采取专利与商标协同的整体知识产权策略。他指出IP密集型产业占欧洲GDP近半，结合双权的企业更易获融资。演讲强调单一专利制度为中小企业提供便捷保护，并提及EPO在AI、量子技术背景下的未来情景规划及与INTA的深化合作。
items.ai_insight_zh: 中国企业应重视专利与商标的组合布局，利用单一专利制度降低进入欧盟市场的成本与风险。鉴于EPO对高质量专利及早期评估的重视，建议优化申请策略以增强法律确定性，吸引资本关注，并密切关注AI领域的IP规则演进。
items.ai_core_points_zh: ["倡导专利与商标协同的整体IP战略，将其嵌入企业商业策略以应对AI加速的创新周期", "强调单一专利制度提升市场准入效率，中小企业占比达40%，凸显其普惠性与竞争力", "展望AI与量子技术下的IP系统演变，深化EPO与INTA在标准必要专利等议题上的对话"]
items.ai_insight_points_zh: ["强化“专利+商标”组合布局，利用单一专利制度高效覆盖欧盟主要市场", "关注EPO对高质量专利的偏好，通过早期评估增强投资吸引力与法律确定性"]

items.title: G 1/25: Oral proceedings before the Enlarged Board of Appeal on description adaptation - GJE
items.title_zh: EPO扩大上诉委员会就说明书适配举行口头审理
items.title_en: EPO Enlarged Board Holds Oral Proceedings in G 1/25 on Description Adaptation
items.summary: Oral proceedings in G 1/25 (“Hydroponics”) took place today before the Enlarged Board of Appeal (EBA) at the European Patent Office (EPO)
items.ai_summary_zh: 欧洲专利局（EPO）扩大上诉委员会于今日就G 1/25号案件（“水培法”）举行口头审理。该案源于T 697/22的提请，核心争议在于欧洲专利说明书是否必须、以及基于何种法律依据进行适应性修改以符合《欧洲专利公约》（EPC）。此程序旨在澄清审查实践中说明书与权利要求一致性要求的法律基础，对欧洲专利申请的撰写及后续审查具有重大指导意义。
items.ai_summary_en: The European Patent Office’s Enlarged Board of Appeal conducted oral proceedings today in case G 1/25, commonly referred to as “Hydroponics.” This referral originates from decision T 697/22 and addresses a critical procedural question: whether and under what legal basis the description of a European patent application must be adapted to align with amended claims. The core issue concerns compliance with the European Patent Convention regarding consistency between claims and description. These proceedings aim to clarify the mandatory standards for description adaptation during examination. The outcome will provide essential guidance on the legal requirements for ensuring that the description adequately supports the claimed subject matter, significantly impacting future patent prosecution strategies and drafting practices before the EPO.
items.ai_insight_zh: 中国企业需密切关注EPO在说明书适配上的最新司法立场。若裁定趋严，申请阶段需确保说明书充分支持权利要求，避免后期因形式缺陷被驳回或限制保护范围。建议优化海外布局策略，加强高质量撰写，预留修改空间以应对潜在的程序性风险。
items.ai_insight_en: This development carries significant implications for patent applicants and rights holders prosecuting cases before the EPO. A stricter interpretation of description adaptation requirements could necessitate more robust initial drafting to ensure full support for potential claim amendments. Applicants should anticipate heightened scrutiny regarding the alignment between descriptive text and claimed subject matter. Proactively addressing these consistency issues during the application phase may mitigate risks of later objections or limitations on protection scope. Ensuring high-quality, comprehensive descriptions now can safeguard against procedural hurdles and enhance the stability of granted patents across European jurisdictions.

items.title: France Design Impact Award 2026
items.title_zh: 法国国家工业产权局公布2026年设计影响力奖40强项目
items.title_en: INPI Reveals 40 Finalists for France Design Impact Award 2026
items.summary: France Design Impact Award 2026 sgurtler@inpi.fr 07 mai 2026 Les 40 projets du prix France Design Impact award 2026 ont été officiellement révélés lors de l’inauguration de la Foire économique de Tours le 2 mai. Cette sélection 2026 met en avant des initiatives venues de toutes les régions de France et démontre le rôle concret du design face aux grands enjeux climatiques et politiques. À travers différentes scènes de vie quotidienne (se soigner, habiter, se déplacer, s’instruire, communiquer, pr
items.ai_summary_zh: 法国国家工业产权局（INPI）于5月2日在图尔经济博览会开幕仪式上正式揭晓“2026法国设计影响力奖”的40个入围项目。该评选由经济、环境和社会三个专家陪审团共同评估，旨在展示设计在应对气候与政治挑战中的具体作用。INPI作为历史合作伙伴参与经济陪审团，强调设计作为经济转型战略杠杆的价值，涵盖产品、服务及品牌视觉等多个领域，凸显了外观设计在商业生态中的核心地位。
items.ai_summary_en: The French National Institute of Industrial Property (INPI) officially announced the 40 finalists for the 2026 France Design Impact Award during the inauguration of the Tours Economic Fair on May 2. The selection highlights initiatives from across all French regions, demonstrating design's concrete role in addressing major climatic and political challenges. Covering daily life scenarios such as healthcare, housing, mobility, education, communication, production, and communal living, the award underscores design as a strategic lever for economic transformation. The projects span product, industrial, brand, visual identity, user interface, spatial, and service design.
items.ai_insight_zh: 中国企业应重视外观设计在欧盟市场的战略价值，不仅限于注册保护，更需结合ESG理念提升设计竞争力。建议关注INPI等机构的设计激励政策，通过高质量设计增强品牌溢价，并积极参与相关国际奖项以提升在欧洲市场的知名度与合规形象。
items.ai_insight_en: This initiative highlights the growing intersection between intellectual property strategy and sustainability metrics within the European design landscape. For rights holders, participation in or monitoring of such awards provides visibility into how national offices value non-traditional IP assets like service and interface design. It signals that commercial impact and ecological vision are increasingly central to official recognition frameworks.

items.title_zh: 首届欧洲发明人网络论坛召开：聚焦激发青年创新活力
items.title_en: The first European Inventor Network Forum
items.summary: On 5 May, members from 12 countries gathered for the inaugural European Inventor Network Forum. Participants exchanged best practices in science communication and strategies to spark curiosity in younger generations.
items.ai_summary_zh: 欧洲专利局（EPO）于5月5日举办首届欧洲发明人网络论坛，汇聚12国成员交流科学传播最佳实践。该网络自2023年成立，旨在通过STEM活动激发青少年发明好奇心，已实施110项活动。论坛探讨了简化复杂科学的方法，并开展专利知识研讨。目前2026年活动招募开放，符合条件的提案可获得2000至6000欧元资助，以支持面向6-18岁学生的创新教育活动。
items.ai_insight_zh: 中国企业应关注EPO对STEM教育的资源倾斜，积极参与相关活动以提升品牌在欧影响力。利用资助机会开展专利科普，有助于构建良好的社区关系，培养潜在的创新人才储备，同时展示企业对欧洲知识产权生态的贡献。

items.title: Tuesday at INTA: How AI is assisting judges | IP’s role in deals
items.title_zh: INTA焦点：AI如何辅助法官裁判及IP在交易中的角色
items.title_en: UK Judges Discuss AI Use in Judgments and IP Role in Corporate Deals at INTA
items.summary: Senior UK judges discussing the impact of AI on the judiciary, and the role of in-house IP lawyers during corporate transactions and carve-outs were among the top talking points
items.ai_summary_zh: 在INTA年会上，英格兰和威尔士高等法院及上诉法院法官讨论了AI在司法中的应用。上诉法院法官Colin Birss透露，他使用AI进行判决的逻辑一致性检查，以发现段落间的矛盾或需重写的部分，但明确反对用AI从头生成判决书。 panel还包括UKIPO指定人员Emma Himsworth。此外，会议还探讨了企业内部IP律师在公司剥离交易中的作用。这反映了英国司法界对AI工具持谨慎辅助态度，强调人类法官的最终责任。
items.ai_summary_en: Senior judges from the England and Wales High Court and Court of Appeal addressed the impact of artificial intelligence on the judiciary during a panel at the INTA Annual Meeting. Lord Justice Colin Birss, alongside Mr Justices Richard Meade and James Mellor, discussed practical applications of AI tools. Lord Justice Birss revealed he uses AI to sense-check judgments for inconsistencies, though he rejects using it to draft decisions from scratch. The session also covered the evolving role of in-house IP counsel in corporate transactions and carve-outs, moderated by Arty Rajendra of Osborne Clarke.
items.ai_insight_zh: 当前更值得关注的是，英国后续是否会围绕这类规则动态给出配套安排或更明确口径。
items.ai_insight_en: This development signals a cautious but growing integration of AI assistance within the English judiciary, specifically for quality control rather than decision-making. For rights holders and litigants, this suggests that judicial reasoning may become more internally consistent due to automated checks. It underscores the continued necessity for precise legal drafting and clear argumentation, as AI tools may highlight ambiguities that judges previously overlooked or resolved manually.

items.ai_summary_zh: 全球最大知识产权会议INTA在伦敦开幕。WIPR提供独家内部洞察，重点关注塑造未来品牌保护的辩论及关键议程。作为行业风向标，会议汇聚全球专家探讨商标战略、执法挑战及政策趋势，对欧盟及英国市场的品牌权利人具有重要参考意义。
items.ai_summary_en: The International Trademark Association (INTA) Annual Meeting has commenced in London, marking the world's largest intellectual property conference. World IP Review provides exclusive on-the-ground coverage, featuring insights from in-house counsel and highlighting key sessions that define the future of brand protection. The event serves as a critical industry barometer, gathering global experts to discuss trademark strategies, enforcement challenges, and policy trends. For rights holders operating within the European Union and United Kingdom markets, these discussions offer significant reference value regarding evolving legal landscapes and strategic brand management approaches in a complex international environment.
items.ai_insight_zh: 中国企业应关注会议关于跨境品牌维权的新趋势，特别是欧盟海关执法力度加强及数字平台责任变化。建议利用此契机对接欧洲律所，评估现有商标布局风险，强化针对恶意抢注的监测机制，以应对日益复杂的海外合规环境。
items.ai_insight_en: Rights holders should closely monitor emerging trends in cross-border brand enforcement, particularly regarding strengthened customs measures within the European Union and shifting liability standards for digital platforms. This gathering offers a strategic opportunity to engage with European law firms for assessing risks associated with current trademark layouts. This established educational program provides a comprehensive overview of EU intellectual property frameworks, offering practitioners essential updates and specialized professional training. The course covers various aspects of EU IP law, serving as a significant academic platform for understanding current legal dynamics. It represents a key opportunity for legal professionals and corporate counsel to deepen their expertise in European IP regulations through structured learning and engagement with leading experts in the field.

items.title: Business and Professional Women Europe visits the EPO
items.title_zh: 欧洲商务与职业女性联盟访问欧洲专利局
items.title_en: EPO and BPW Europe Discuss Inclusive Innovation and Women in STEM
items.summary: On 24 April 2026, members of the EU branch of the International Federation of Business & Professional Women (BPW) were welcomed at the EPO.
items.ai_summary_zh: 2026年4月24日，欧洲商务与职业女性联盟（BPW Europe）代表团访问慕尼黑欧洲专利局（EPO）。双方就构建包容性创新生态系统达成共识，强调女性在STEM领域的重要性。EPO副总裁Nellie Simon介绍了支持女性人才发展的举措，包括青年专业人员计划及2028年女性员工占比达40%的目标。会议期间还探讨了专利对初创企业和中小企业的益处，旨在通过多元化视角推动创新资源的有效配置。
items.ai_summary_en: On 24 April 2026, a delegation from Business and Professional Women (BPW) Europe visited the European Patent Office (EPO) headquarters in Munich. The visit focused on fostering an inclusive innovation ecosystem, emphasizing the critical role of women in science, technology, engineering, and mathematics (STEM). EPO Vice-President Nellie Simon highlighted shared commitments to gender equality, outlining initiatives such as the Young Professionals Programme and Girl's Day. She reaffirmed the Office’s target of achieving at least 40% female representation in its workforce by 2028. Discussions also covered the strategic value of patents for startups and small and medium-sized enterprises (SMEs), aiming to optimize resource allocation through diverse perspectives and support broader innovation goals.
items.ai_insight_zh: 中国企业应关注EPO对多样性及中小企业支持的倾斜政策。利用EPO针对SME的专项服务可降低海外布局成本。同时，重视ESG中的性别平等指标，有助于提升在欧洲市场的品牌形象与合规竞争力，契合当地主流价值观。
items.ai_insight_en: Market participants should note the EPO’s increasing emphasis on diversity, equity, and inclusion within the European innovation landscape. Aligning corporate strategies with these values can enhance brand reputation and regulatory compliance in EU markets. Companies should actively utilize specialized EPO services designed for SMEs to optimize patent filing costs and streamline international portfolio management. Furthermore, integrating gender equality metrics into environmental, social, and governance (ESG) frameworks is becoming essential for maintaining competitive advantage and meeting local stakeholder expectations regarding social responsibility and inclusive growth.

items.title: 26 april 2026: World Intellectual Property Day
items.title_zh: 2026年世界知识产权日：聚焦体育与商标
items.title_en: BOIP Highlights Intellectual Property and Sports for World IP Day 2026
items.summary: 26 April 2026 is World Intellectual Property Day. And this year, we are focusing on intellectual property and sports.
items.ai_summary_zh: 比荷卢知识产权局（BOIP）宣布2026年4月26日世界知识产权日的主题为“知识产权与体育”。该机构旨在通过此主题提高公众对体育产业中知识产权保护重要性的认识，并邀请公众在比利时、荷兰和卢森堡识别与体育相关的商标注册案例。此举意在展示体育品牌如何通过商标权保护其商业价值与创新成果，强调IP在体育生态中的关键作用。
items.ai_summary_en: The Benelux Office for Intellectual Property (BOIP) has designated 'Intellectual Property and Sports' as the central theme for World Intellectual Property Day on 26 April 2026. This initiative aims to elevate public awareness regarding the critical role of intellectual property protection within the sports industry. The office invites stakeholders to identify sports-related trademark registrations across Belgium, the Netherlands, and Luxembourg. By highlighting these examples, BOIP seeks to demonstrate how trademarks safeguard commercial value and innovation in sports branding. The campaign underscores the essential function of IP rights in maintaining the integrity and economic viability of the broader sports ecosystem, encouraging market participants to recognize and respect brand assets in this dynamic sector.
items.ai_insight_zh: 中国体育品牌出海应重视比荷卢市场的商标布局，特别是针对赛事名称、吉祥物及运动装备的注册。建议企业提前进行商标检索，避免侵权风险，并利用此次宣传契机加强品牌合规管理，确保在欧盟核心市场的知识产权资产安全与商业竞争力。
items.ai_insight_en: Market participants operating in or entering the Benelux region should prioritize comprehensive trademark strategies for sports-related assets. This includes securing registrations for event names, mascots, and athletic equipment to prevent infringement risks. Companies are advised to conduct thorough prior art searches before launching new brands or campaigns. Leveraging global awareness days like World IP Day provides an opportunity to review internal compliance frameworks. Proactive management ensures that intellectual property assets remain secure, thereby supporting long-term commercial competitiveness and brand integrity within key European markets.

items.ai_summary_en: The IPKat reports that the Pharma & Biotech Patent Litigation in Europe conference is scheduled to take place in Amsterdam over two days. This event is designed for legal professionals and industry experts interested in the intersection of pharmaceuticals, biotechnology, and intellectual property rights. The primary objective is to provide an in-depth analysis of recent case law within the pharma sector, specifically addressing patent disputes relevant to the pharmaceutical and biotechnology industries. As a media partner, The IPKat offers readers a special 10% discount on registration fees using the code D10-999-IPKAT26. The conference serves as a vital platform for networking and exchanging insights on cutting-edge legal practices, helping attendees stay informed about critical developments in European patent litigation strategies.

By engaging with these high-level discussions, legal teams can refine their global patent portfolios and litigation strategies. items.title: World IP Day 2026: IP and Sports: Ready, Set, Innovate
items.title_zh: 2026年世界知识产权日：体育与IP协同创新
items.title_en: EPO Announces World IP Day 2026 Theme: IP and Sports Innovation
items.summary: EPO and national offices celebrate IP-driven innovation in sport
items.ai_summary_zh: 欧洲专利局（EPO）宣布2026年4月26日世界知识产权日主题为“IP与体育：准备、设定、创新”。该活动旨在强调知识产权在保护现代体育技术、设计及品牌中的关键作用，涵盖先进装备、性能提升技术及赛事转播等领域。EPO将与各国知识产权局合作开展社交媒体活动，通过#WorldIPDay标签分享全球激励运动员和连接粉丝的创新故事，展示IP对体育产业的支撑价值。
items.ai_summary_en: The European Patent Office (EPO) has announced that the global intellectual property community will celebrate World IP Day 2026 on 26 April under the theme 'IP and Sports: Ready, Set, Innovate'. This initiative highlights the critical role of IP rights in safeguarding the technologies, designs, and brands that define modern sports. The campaign emphasizes how IP protection supports advancements in athletic equipment, performance-enhancing technologies, and broadcast media. To mark the occasion, the EPO is collaborating with national IP offices to launch a coordinated social media campaign. Participants are encouraged to follow the conversation using the #WorldIPDay hashtag to discover stories illustrating how IP motivates athletes and connects fans worldwide.
items.ai_insight_zh: 当前更值得关注的是，欧洲层面后续是否会围绕这类规则动态给出配套安排或更明确口径。
items.ai_insight_en: This annual thematic focus provides context for understanding current policy priorities regarding innovation in specific sectors. For rights holders in the sports technology and apparel sectors, this alignment offers an opportunity to review portfolio visibility and brand positioning. The emphasis on digital engagement and storytelling suggests a continued trend towards public-facing IP education, which may influence how offices communicate the value of intangible assets to non-specialist audiences.

items.title: Forum européen de la propriété intellectuelle (FEPI) 2026
items.title_zh: 2026 年欧洲知识产权论坛：全球反假冒斗争的核心
items.title_en: 2026 European IP Forum: Geopolitics and Technology Reshape Anti-Counterfeiting Strategies
items.summary: Forum européen de la propriété intellectuelle (FEPI) 2026 sgurtler@inpi.fr 30 mars 2026 Le FEPI au cœur de la lutte mondiale anti-contrefaçon Organisé par l’Unifab autour du thème « Géopolitique, législations, technologies… et même vocabulaire ! Quelles nouvelles influences sur la propriété intellectuelle ? », le forum, devenu au fil des ans incontournable et reconnu à l’échelle internationale, a une nouvelle fois réuni des intervenants de renom devant un parterre de dirigeants de PME et de prof
items.ai_summary_zh: 由 Unifab 组织的 2026 年欧洲知识产权论坛在巴黎举行，聚焦地缘政治、立法及技术对知识产权的新影响。法国工业产权局（INPI）作为历史合作伙伴参会，总局长 Pascal Faure 强调需协助企业将无形资产保护纳入发展战略，以培育欧洲冠军企业，并探讨了优化知识产权获取路径的潜在改革方向。
items.ai_summary_en: Organized by UNIFAB, the 2026 European Intellectual Property Forum (FEPI) convened in Paris to address how geopolitical shifts, legislative changes, and technological advancements influence intellectual property protection. The event featured seven panel discussions focusing on global anti-counterfeiting efforts and cross-border innovation practices. Pascal Faure, Director General of the French National Institute of Industrial Property (INPI), emphasized the critical need for companies to integrate intangible asset protection into their core development strategies. He highlighted INPI's ongoing commitment to supporting businesses and exploring reforms to facilitate easier access to IP systems, reinforcing the institute's role in fostering European champions through robust IP valorization.
items.ai_insight_zh: 当前更值得关注的是，法国后续是否会围绕这类规则动态给出配套安排或更明确口径。
items.ai_insight_en: For rights holders and in-house counsel, this forum underscores the increasing intersection of macro-political trends with operational IP enforcement. The emphasis on integrating IP into broader business strategy suggests a shift towards viewing IP not merely as a legal shield but as a central component of corporate valuation and market expansion. Stakeholders should note the institutional focus on facilitating access to IP mechanisms, which may signal upcoming administrative or procedural adjustments in France and potentially across the EU.

items.summary: MediaKat freedomThe Katfriends at the Academy of European Law (ERA) in Trier are always busy organizing new activities and events in various legal fields. This time, they wish IPKat readers to know the forthcoming Annual Conference on European Media Law, which will be in Trier and online on 7 and 8 May 2026.In its 2026 Work Programme, the European Commission announced a revision of a key
items.ai_summary_zh: 欧洲法学院（ERA）将于 2026 年 5 月在德国特里尔及线上举办欧洲媒体法年度会议。会议背景是欧盟委员会在 2026 年工作规划中宣布将修订关键媒体法律框架。该活动旨在探讨最新立法动向，并为 IPKat 读者提供专属参会折扣，聚焦欧盟层面的媒体与知识产权政策协调。
items.ai_summary_en: The Academy of European Law (ERA) will host its Annual Conference on European Media Law in Trier, Germany, and online on 7–8 May 2026. This event coincides with the European Commission’s 2026 Work Programme, which announces a revision of key media law frameworks. The conference aims to explore these latest legislative developments and facilitate discussion on the coordination of media and intellectual property policies at the EU level. IPKat readers are eligible for a specific discount to attend this hybrid event, providing an opportunity to engage directly with experts on the evolving regulatory landscape affecting digital content and platform responsibilities across Europe.
items.ai_insight_zh: 欧盟媒体法修订将直接影响数字内容版权合规。中国企业应关注新规对平台责任及内容授权的影响，提前调整在欧洲的内容分发策略，并利用此类会议洞察立法趋势以规避合规风险。
items.ai_insight_en: The upcoming revision of EU media laws will significantly impact digital content copyright compliance and platform liability standards. Rights holders and market participants should closely monitor how these regulatory changes affect content licensing obligations and distribution strategies within the European market. Attending industry conferences like this one provides valuable insights into legislative trends, allowing stakeholders to anticipate compliance requirements and adjust their operational frameworks proactively. This approach helps mitigate legal risks associated with new media regulations while ensuring alignment with evolving EU intellectual property policies.

items.title: [Guest Post] [Conference Report] Intellectual Property in the Gaming Industry (JDPI)
items.title_zh: 瑞士日内瓦大学举办游戏行业知识产权年度会议报告
items.title_en: JDPI Conference Report: Intellectual Property Challenges and Strategies in the Video Game Industry
items.summary: The IPKat has received and is pleased to host this guest report on the annual IP conference, Journée de droit de la propriété intellectuelle (JDPI), which focused on the video game industry this year. The report is provided by Marion Consoli (PhD Candidate at the University of Geneva) et Léonard Diambrini Palazzi (Trainee Lawyer at MLL Legal).Here’s what Marion and Léonard
items.ai_summary_zh: 本文报道了由日内瓦大学主办的年度知识产权法律日（JDPI）会议，今年重点聚焦视频游戏行业的知识产权挑战。来自学术界与实务界的专家探讨了版权、许可及新兴技术对游戏产业的影响，旨在促进欧洲特别是瑞士地区对该领域法律问题的深入理解与交流。
items.ai_summary_en: This report covers the annual Journée de droit de la propriété intellectuelle (JDPI) conference, hosted by the University of Geneva. The event specifically addressed intellectual property challenges within the video game sector. Experts from academia and legal practice convened to examine critical issues including copyright protection, licensing frameworks, and the impact of emerging technologies on game development. The discussions aimed to deepen understanding and foster dialogue regarding these complex legal matters, particularly within Switzerland and the broader European context. By bringing together diverse stakeholders, the conference highlighted the evolving nature of IP rights in digital entertainment and the necessity for robust legal strategies to protect creative assets in this rapidly changing industry landscape.
items.ai_insight_zh: 中国游戏企业出海欧洲应关注瑞士及周边国家版权合规，提前布局本地化许可机制，防范侵权风险并提升维权能力。
items.ai_insight_en: Market participants operating in Europe should prioritize copyright compliance within Switzerland and neighboring jurisdictions. Establishing localized licensing mechanisms early is essential to mitigate infringement risks and enhance enforcement capabilities. Rights holders must conduct thorough compliance reviews for game content distributed across European markets. Developing cross-border cooperation frameworks helps navigate the complex authorization environment. Monitoring legislative developments and judicial interpretations in key European hubs ensures that IP strategies remain aligned with regional legal standards, thereby safeguarding commercial interests and facilitating smoother market entry for digital entertainment products.

Partenaire engagé des entrepreneurs à chaque étape de leur parcours, l’INPI sera une nouvelle fois au rendez-vous. Vous avez des questions sur le dépôt de votre marque ou la protection de vos innovations et de votre design ? Vous souhaitez des renseignements sur les formalités d’ent
items.ai_summary_zh: 法国国家工业产权局（INPI）确认参加 2026 年 4 月在巴黎拉德芳斯竞技场举办的第 33 届“创业者大会”。作为企业合作伙伴，INPI 将在 B51 展位提供关于商标申请、创新及外观设计保护的免费咨询，并举办专题研讨会，指导企业如何进行工业产权诊断、品牌保护及网站命名策略，以协助初创企业完善知识产权布局。
items.ai_summary_en: The French National Institute of Industrial Property (INPI) will participate in the 33rd edition of Go Entrepreneurs, held on April 15–16, 2026, at Paris La Défense Arena. As a committed partner for entrepreneurs, INPI experts will be available at stand B51 to provide free individual consultations regarding trademark filing, innovation protection, and industrial property diagnostics. The institute will also host specific workshops and roundtables focusing on brand protection strategies, website naming conventions, and the strategic role of intellectual property in business development.

items.ai_insight_en: For rights holders and applicants targeting the French market, this event offers direct access to official guidance on local registration formalities and brand strategy. Engaging with INPI representatives can clarify procedural requirements for trademarks and designs early in the project lifecycle. Participants should note the specific focus on aligning brand names with digital presence, which is increasingly relevant for comprehensive IP portfolio management in Europe.

items.title: IPEP Presented in Athens: Strengthening IP Enforcement Through Digital Tools
items.title_zh: EUIPO 于雅典展示 IPEP：以数字工具强化知识产权保护
items.title_en: EUIPO Showcases IPEP in Athens: Enhancing IP Enforcement via Digital Tools
items.summary: IPEP Presented in Athens: Strengthening IP Enforcement Through Digital Tools
items.ai_summary_zh: 欧盟知识产权局（EUIPO）在希腊雅典联合当地市场监管机构及全球反假冒组织，举办针对一线执法人员的培训活动。会议重点展示了知识产权执法门户（IPEP）及其移动应用，旨在通过数字化手段实现权利人与执法部门间的实时信息交换，提升对汽车零件、时尚及烟草等假冒商品的查获效率，并计划于 2026 至 2027 年深化在希腊的合作与推广。
items.ai_summary_en: The European Union Intellectual Property Office (EUIPO) presented the IP Enforcement Portal (IPEP) during a specialized training event in Athens, Greece. Co-organized with the Greek Independent Authority for Market Control and Consumer’s Protection and the Global Anti-Counterfeiting Group, the session targeted frontline officers responsible for detecting counterfeit goods. The initiative highlights the EUIPO’s strategy to strengthen intellectual property enforcement through digital innovation. By facilitating secure, real-time data exchange between rights holders and authorities, IPEP aims to improve the efficiency of seizures involving high-risk categories such as automotive parts, fashion apparel, and tobacco products. This collaboration underscores a broader commitment to expanding digital enforcement capabilities across EU member states, ensuring that national authorities are equipped with modern tools to combat market infringement effectively.
items.ai_insight_zh: 中国出口企业应关注欧盟数字化执法趋势，主动利用 IPEP 平台备案知识产权。建议企业配合当地执法部门提供真伪鉴别技术支持，建立快速响应机制，以降低产品在希腊及欧盟市场的被侵权风险。
items.ai_insight_en: Market participants should monitor the increasing integration of digital platforms like IPEP into EU customs and enforcement workflows. Rights holders are encouraged to proactively register their intellectual property portfolios on the portal to facilitate faster identification and seizure of infringing goods by authorities. Establishing direct technical support channels for local enforcement agencies can significantly reduce response times during inspections. As digital enforcement becomes standard practice, maintaining up-to-date product authentication data on official platforms is critical for effective brand protection strategies within the Greek and wider European markets.

Training covered counterfeit identification techniques and shared practical experiences from rights holders.
Stakeholders committed to deepening cooperation and promoting IPEP adoption across Greece in 2026-2027.

items.summary: The IPKat blog reports on copyright, patent, trade mark, info-tech and confidentiality issues from a mainly UK and European perspective.
items.ai_summary_zh: 英国特许专利代理人协会（CIPA）正为其权威指南《专利法黑皮书》最新版招募作者，重点涵盖侵权诉讼、专利撤销及统一专利法院（UPC）实施等章节。同时，编辑团队向业界征集内容修订建议，旨在确保该指南准确反映包括单一专利制度在内的最新法律动态，以满足专业需求。
items.ai_insight_zh: 中国企业应关注此指南更新，因其深刻影响在英及通过 UPC 的专利策略。建议利用反馈渠道表达诉求，并密切跟踪新版中关于统一专利法院的实务指引，以优化在欧洲的专利布局与风险应对。

items.ai_summary_zh: IPKat 发布本周知识产权动态，重点预告由理查德·阿诺德爵士主讲的公开讲座。该活动将于 2026 年 2 月 26 日由伦敦城市圣乔治大学举办，主题聚焦英国与欧盟版权制度的优势与劣势分析。此举旨在促进学界与业界对后脱欧时代两地版权法律差异及协调问题的深入理解与交流。
items.ai_summary_en: The IPKat has released its weekly intellectual property update, highlighting an upcoming public lecture delivered by Sir Richard Arnold. Scheduled for February 26, 2026, the event is hosted by City St George's in London. The presentation focuses on a comparative analysis of the advantages and disadvantages inherent in both the United Kingdom and European Union copyright frameworks. This initiative aims to foster deeper understanding and dialogue among academic and industry stakeholders regarding the legal divergences and coordination challenges that have emerged in the post-Brexit era. It serves as a key opportunity for professionals to engage with critical developments affecting cross-border copyright protection and enforcement strategies across these two significant jurisdictions.
items.ai_insight_zh: 中国企业应关注英欧版权制度分化趋势，提前评估合规成本。建议利用此类高端学术活动洞察司法风向，优化在英及欧盟市场的版权布局与维权策略，规避因规则差异导致的法律风险。
items.ai_insight_en: Market participants should closely monitor the growing divergence between UK and EU copyright rules to accurately assess dual compliance costs. Leveraging insights from high-level academic discourse allows rights holders to refine their copyright portfolios and enforcement strategies within both regions. Proactive engagement with such expert analyses helps mitigate legal risks arising from regulatory discrepancies. Companies operating across borders must remain agile, adapting their IP management practices to address the evolving judicial and legislative environment. This strategic approach ensures robust protection while minimizing exposure to potential litigation or regulatory penalties stemming from misaligned compliance efforts in distinct legal territories.
